zh.Shalbourne Churchyard, Shalbourne, Berks: 英國英格蘭維爾爾沙爾布恩沙爾布恩 亞德
zh.lifeSummary: John Briant於大約1572年出生在英國維爾爾沙爾布恩。他於大約1596年與Alice在英國波克夏結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1643年6月20日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾沙爾布恩沙爾布恩 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Briant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Laure.

variants.name: Bryant
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Celtic personal name Brian (from brigo- ‘high’ + the suffix -ant-), with excrescent -t. Breton bearers of this name were among the Normans who invaded England in 1066. They went on to settle in Ireland in the 12th century, where the name mingled with the native Irish form Briain (see O'Brien ). The latter had also been borrowed, as Brján, by the Vikings, who introduced it independently into northwestern England before the Norman Conquest.
Breton: very rare variant of   Briant   (see   Briand  ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The American poet William Cullen Bryant (1794–1878) came of a New England family, being descended from Stephen Bryant, who had settled in Plymouth Colony in 1632.

variants.name: Briand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Jacques, Alban, Alphonse, Andre, Benoit, Florent, Francois, Olivier.

Breton (rarely Le Briand): from Old Breton brient ‘privilege, prerogative’ (a derivative of bri ‘dignity, consideration’), used as a medieval personal name and as a nickname (possibly for a pretentious individual). Compare Briant , Brien , Brillant , and Pontbriand .

History: Pierre-François Briand/Brillant from Saint-Malo in Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany (France), married Renée Marchand in Port-Toulouse (St. Peters), NS, Canada, c. 1730. Thomas Briand/Briant from Pleudihen-sur-Rance in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marie-Josèphe Lefebvre in Quebec City, QC, in 1754.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: John Briant was born about 1572, in Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England. He married Alice about 1596, in Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died before 20 June 1643, in his hometown, and was buried in Shalbourne Churchyard, Shalbourne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: John Briant(은)는 대략 1572년 영국 윌츠히르 샤보우른에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1596년 영국 버크실르에서 Alice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1643년 6월 20일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 샤보우른 샤보우른 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.
